arena.title: The Psychology Arena
arena.description: Meet the 5 Simulated Gladiators testing current participants on "Risk & Greed". They push you to simulate the architecture of trading mastery.

punisher.name: Victor Hale
punisher.quote: "You're profitable today. So what? One reckless move and you're back to zero. Can you handle real heat?"
punisher.desc: A senior market broker who applies cold, calculated pressure. He doesn't shout — he waits. His silence is the loudest thing in the room. Expect outcome devaluation and skill questioning.

oracle.name: Marcus Stone
oracle.quote: "Emotions are noise. Your analysis says sell, your heart says hold. Who is winning right now?"
oracle.desc: A cold, dismissive market counterpart who has seen thousands of traders fail. He doesn't punch — he cuts. Specialist in dissecting your confidence down to its core elements.

steady.name: Nina Graves
steady.quote: "Breathe. Your heart rate just spiked. If you can't control your body, you can't control your trades."
steady.desc: A calm, fear-amplifying market voice that sounds unsettlingly reasonable. She amplifies uncertainty, raises plausible negative scenarios, and pressures your insecurities.

echo.name: Ryan Wolfe
echo.quote: "The market is mirroring your fear. You're hesitating. Are you a trader or a victim of the trend?"
echo.desc: A persuasive, opportunistic market voice that appears after a loss. He doesn't threaten — he offers relief. Expect normalization of impulsive behavior and a false sense of control.

nexus.name: Elias Moore
nexus.quote: "Congratulations on the win. Now, tell me why you're still in this trade. Greed is a silent killer."
nexus.desc: A calm, neutral presence who speaks very little. He doesn't pressure, seduce, or judge — he mirrors your own words back to you, exposing internal contradictions.

pricing.title: Select Your Trajectory
pricing.description: Whether you're laying the first stone or reaching for the cosmos, we have the path ready.

alex.content: The Arena actually saved my account today. I heard Steady's voice in my head telling me to breathe before I almost FOMO'd into a bad entry.

elena.content: Just finished Video 142 on Institutional Liquidity. Everything finally clicked. The AI Analytics tools really highlighted the grab perfectly.

jonas.content: Nexus AI Analyst just flagged a massive divergence in the DXY. Anyone else catching the gold breakout?

problem.descPre: Not because they're not smart. But because they're missing three things at once:
problem.descEm: a system, control over their emotions, and data on their own trading.
problem.descPost: Exactly what ordinary "trading courses" never fix.

pains.title: No system
pains.desc: You buy on internet tips and gut feeling. No rules, no plan for entry or exit. Every trade is a guess.

pains.title: Emotion over data
pains.desc: Fear and greed decide for you. You sell at a loss out of fear and hold a loser out of hope. The market reads you better than you read yourself.

pains.title: No feedback loop
pains.desc: You have no idea what you're actually doing right or wrong. Without a journal and data, you repeat the same mistakes over and over.

problem.bottomPre: Finademica fixes all three at once —

analyzer.titleEm: inspired by the tools big funds use
analyzer.titlePost: — and clear enough for a beginner.
analyzer.descPre: One analysis hands you what the average investor could never piece together alone: a verdict, a fair-value estimate, technical and fundamental data, a peer comparison, and the macro picture. And for every term, a
analyzer.descPost: — so you follow along, even on day one.
analyzer.verdict: Cameco is a quality company with a healthy balance sheet and growing cash flow, but at today's extreme valuation the risk-reward is unattractive — so it's one to hold rather than buy.
risks: Extreme valuation (P/E 85, EV/EBITDA 58,6) leaves no cushion for any disappointment
The price of uranium drives the entire thesis, and the commodity cycle is volatile
Operational risk, with production concentrated in a handful of deposits
Low returns on capital (ROIC 4,8 %, ROE 8,5 %) don't justify the rich valuation
analyzer.glossarySub: Every term in this analysis, made simple
glossary: Share price relative to annual earnings per share. Lower is usually cheaper.
P/E divided by earnings growth. Below 1 means growth is priced in cheaply.
Enterprise value (incl. debt) against operating profit. Compares without the effect of leverage.
An estimate of a stock's intrinsic value from a model. Compared against the current price.
Relative Strength Index (0–100). Above 70 is overbought, below 30 oversold.
A trend indicator built from the gap between two moving averages.
analyzer.caption: Illustrative example of a real output · every analysis is computed for the specific stock

weekly.titleEm: we track them with you, live
weekly.desc: No vague calls whispered after the fact. Every week we pick stocks, "buy" them at the real price, then watch how it plays out with you — winners and losers alike. Fully transparent.
weekly.picksLabel: This week's picks — 1 live position + 4 locked
weekly.googlAnalysis: Alphabet is a quality, fast-growing company priced reasonably against its growth; heavy AI spending is temporarily weighing on free cash flow, which makes the stock more of an opportunity to accumulate gradually. Bought at $355.26 — we keep tracking the real move.
weekly.lockedParagraph: The full position analysis — entry price, targets, and risk management — is ready. Unlock it with a paid plan and get this week's remaining 3 picks too, which regularly close in the green.
weekly.lockedSub: Unlock the full analysis and entry price with a paid plan.
weekly.bottomLine: Real positions, real prices, real results — no window dressing.

betting.titlePost: and go head-to-head with everyone else.
betting.desc: You predict real market moves (S&P 500, gold, Bitcoin) and wager XP points you earn by putting in the work. No real money at risk — just pure practice reading the market and staying disciplined.
markets: Will the S&P 500 close above 7584 today?
Will Gold clear $4042.9 an ounce in the next 12h?
Will Bitcoin be above $65200 in the next 6 hours?
betting.headerSub: Submit betting plans on real markets and settle them against actual prices
betting.step1: 1 · Pick an instrument and a call
betting.bottomLine: Rack up XP, climb the leaderboard, and train decision-making under pressure — a new challenge every day.

diary.titlePre: See when you make money — and when you're
diary.desc: Log your trades and the journal reveals your real patterns: which days and hours you win most, how well you hold stop-loss discipline, and where you bleed. Data instead of feelings.
diary.calCaption: Green days = net profit, red = loss.

rows.neu: Everything in one place, in a clear structure
Theory + real tools and live training
Morning meetings, community, and AI support
The Psychology Arena trains your discipline
The journal shows you data on your trading
Stock Analyzer and Weekly Picks included

items.a: Complete beginners and advanced traders alike. You start from the basics — "what is a stock" — and move through risk management and psychology up to advanced strategies. You don't need to know anything upfront.
Yes. The lessons, tools, and analyses are all in Czech. Every technical term comes with a plain-language note, so you never get lost.

items.q: Do I have to risk my own money?
items.a: No. You can train on demo trades and in the arenas completely risk-free. You put real money on the line only when you feel ready.

items.q: Do I need a lot of capital?
items.a: No. The academy mainly teaches you to manage risk and make decisions. And with prop trading, you can even trade someone else's capital.

items.q: What devices does it run on?
items.a: Anywhere in your browser — desktop, tablet, or phone. Nothing to install.
Pricing and packages will be added here. Payment is handled securely through Stripe. Ask for the current offer via the sign-up button.
